title: pelargonium × haemastictum
intro: Pelargonium × haemastictum, also known as the Cape Rose or Cold Infusion, is a hybrid plant species within the Pelargonium genus, native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic compounds. The medicinal properties of Pelargonium × haemastictum have been associated with various health benefits, including relief from fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and reduce stress. Pelargonium × haemastictum can be consumed as a herbal preparation, typically in the form of a cold infusion or tea, allowing the bioactive compounds to be released and utilized by the body.
